steps.title: Use the solved-conjecture contrast
steps.content: The phrase though it seemed sets up a contrast with the fact that the conjecture eventually yielded to a proof. The blank should describe something that seemed impossible to overcome, making the later proof surprising.

steps.title: Reject weak contrasts
steps.content: B means clear or certain, which does not contrast with being proved. C means faultless, which also creates no useful contrast. D means inevitable, making the proof expected rather than surprising. The answer is A.
